Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The event log has 3 cases. Case 1 trace: RA -> VF -> AE -> RMI -> VF -> AD -> SC -> AC Case 2 trace: RA -> VF -> AE -> AD -> SC -> AC Case 3 trace: RA -> VF -> AE -> RMI -> RMI -> VF -> AD -> SC -> AC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: All traces start with RA. Then VF. Then AE.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After AE, theres an optional loop of RMI followed by VF. Wait, in Case 3: RMI -> RMI -> VF.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: If A is VF, then after AE, we have a loop where A = VF, B = RMI. Lets trace * ( VF, RMI ):

Name: Validation - NE
Text: This does NOT match * (VF, RMI). How can we cover VF, then RMI, then RMI, then VF?

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Lets see: * (RMI, tau) means: RMI, then either exit or tau -> RMI. What if the loop is * (VF, RMI) but we allow RMI to loop on itself? No, we cant.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: after AE, its an XOR: Path 1: AD Path 2: -> ( * (RMI, tau), VF, AD )

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: X ( tau, -> ( * (RMI, tau), VF ) ) If we choose tau: sequence is tau -> AD. Which is just AD. If we choose -> ( * (RMI, tau), VF ): * (RMI, tau) can be: RMI; or RMI -> tau -> RMI; etc. Then it does VF.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This perfectly matches! Case 1: AE -> Path 2 (1 RMI) -> VF -> AD. Case 2: AE -> Path 1 -> AD. Case 3: AE -> Path 2 (2 RMIs) -> VF -> AD.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: * ( tau, RMI ) means: execute tau (silent step), then either exit, or execute RMI and then tau again. So it generates: tau, tau -> RMI -> tau, tau -> RMI -> tau -> RMI -> tau. This is exactly equivalent to 0 or more RMIs!

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The first one X ( tau, -> ( * ( RMI, tau ), VF ) ) is simpler and MORE precise. ... Total 6 nodes.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets trace X ( tau, -> ( * ( RMI, tau ), VF ) ) again. Case 2: X chooses tau. ... Case 3: X chooses -> ( * ( RMI, tau ), VF ). * executes RMI, then tau, then RMI, then exits. Then VF. Remaining trace: RMI, RMI, VF.
